content: Quant-finance backtest biases translate to sports betting. Look-ahead bias = info not available at decision time (closing odds, final line movement, post-event results) silently inflates ROI. Survivorship bias = only keeping still-active markets, dropping voided/cancelled events. Hindsight/data snooping = params tuned to known outcomes. Odds-snapshot selection bias = picking best odds from a range you could not bet at. Biggest lever: point-in-time/as-of data discipline, fix decision-time snapshot, never re-select post-hoc.
